steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes Wladyslaw I Herman as 'well-documented' and Lestek with the phrase 'whose historical actuality is disputed.' The correct choice must reflect exactly this contrast between the two figures.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Historians believe that the *Gesta principum Polonorum* provides more evidence for Lestek's existence than it does for Wladyslaw I Herman's existence.: the passage says the opposite — Lestek relies on oral tradition, not documented records. ✗
  • Choice B — Historians agree that Wladyslaw I Herman existed, but disagree about whether Lestek existed.: directly matches 'well-documented' vs. 'historical actuality is disputed.' ✓
  • Choice C — Historians agree that Lestek ruled Poland much later than Wladyslaw I Herman.: reverses the timeline — Lestek ruled in the 9th–10th centuries; Wladyslaw ruled 1079–1102. ✗
  • Choice D — Historians find the orally transmitted stories affirming the existence of Wladyslaw I Herman to be more convincing than similar stories about Lestek.: the passage distinguishes Lestek's oral tradition from Wladyslaw's documented records. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
